meta.title: ICH Q1 Stability Requirements — Consolidated Extraction
meta.scope: ICH Q1A(R2), Q1B, Q1C, Q1D, Q1E, Q1F (withdrawn), WHO TRS 1010 Annex 10, ICH Q1 2025 Step 2 Draft
meta.note: Machine-readable rules for automated stability data evaluation in CTD Module 3.2.P.8 and 3.2.S.7

Q1A_R2.title: Stability Testing of New Drug Substances and Products
Q1A_R2.scope: New molecular entities and associated drug products; excludes abbreviated/abridged applications, clinical trial applications, biotechnological/biological products (see Q5C), and new dosage forms of approved actives (see Q1C)

Q1B.title: Stability Testing: Photostability Testing of New Drug Substances and Products
Q1B.scope: Annex to Q1A; photostability of new molecular entities and drug products

Q1C.title: Stability Testing for New Dosage Forms
Q1C.scope: New dosage forms of already-approved active substances by the same applicant

Q1D.title: Bracketing and Matrixing Designs for Stability Testing of New Drug Substances and Products
Q1D.scope: Reduced stability study designs for drug products with multiple strengths, container sizes, or fills

Q1E.title: Evaluation for Stability Data
Q1E.scope: Statistical evaluation of stability data, extrapolation rules, shelf life estimation

Q1F_withdrawn.title: Stability Data Package for Registration in Climatic Zones III and IV
Q1F_withdrawn.scope: Withdrawn due to divergence in Zone IV requirements; replaced by WHO TRS 1010 Annex 10 and regional guidelines
Q1F_withdrawn.replacement: WHO Technical Report Series No. 1010, Annex 10 (2018)

Q1_2025_draft.title: ICH Q1 Stability Studies for Drug Substances and Drug Products
Q1_2025_draft.scope: Consolidates Q1A(R2), Q1B, Q1C, Q1D, Q1E, and Q5C into a single guideline; adds ATMPs, enhanced modelling, in-use stability, holding times, short-term storage
Q1_2025_draft.status: Step 2 public comment; Step 4 target late 2026/2027

long_term.note: If this option is selected, intermediate testing at 30C/65%RH is required when significant change occurs at accelerated
No intermediate condition when this is the long-term condition
WHO-specified for hot/very humid climates; no intermediate condition

intermediate.trigger: Required when long-term is 25C/60%RH AND significant change occurs at accelerated within 6 months
intermediate.note: Not applicable when long-term is 30C/65%RH or 30C/75%RH

freezer.excursion_testing: Single batch at 5C +/- 3C or 25C +/- 2C to address short-term excursions

intermediate.trigger: Required when long-term is 25C/60%RH AND significant change at accelerated within 6 months

impermeable_containers.note: Humidity not a concern; stability studies at any controlled or ambient humidity
impermeable_containers.examples: Glass ampoules, aluminum tubes, sealed aluminum cans

semi_permeable_containers.significant_change_water_loss: 5% loss in water from initial value after equivalent of 3 months at 40C/NMT 25% RH
semi_permeable_containers.small_containers: For containers 1 mL or less or unit-dose: 5% water loss acceptable if justified

zone_I.note: WHO-defined; ICH regions use Zone II conditions

zone_IVb.note: Adopted by ASEAN, Brazil, and others after Q1F withdrawal

shelf_life_lte_12_months.thereafter: Every 3 months through proposed shelf life

accelerated.note: If results likely to approach significant change, add 4th time point or additional samples at final

drug_substance.container_closure: Same as or simulating proposed storage/distribution packaging

drug_product.scale: At least 2 of 3 at pilot scale; 3rd can be smaller if justified
drug_product.formulation: Same formulation and container closure as proposed for marketing
drug_product.drug_substance_batches: Use different drug substance batches where possible
drug_product.each_strength_and_size: Each individual strength and container size tested unless bracketing/matrixing applied

criteria.note: Or failure to meet acceptance criteria for potency when using biological/immunological procedures

criteria.threshold: Any degradation product exceeding its acceptance criterion

criteria.examples: Color, phase separation, resuspendibility, caking, hardness, dose delivery per actuation
criteria.exceptions: Softening of suppositories and melting of creams may be expected under accelerated conditions

criteria.threshold: Failure to meet acceptance criterion (as appropriate for dosage form)
Failure to meet acceptance criteria for 12 dosage units
5% loss from initial value after equivalent of 3 months at 40C/NMT 25% RH

accelerated_exceptions_not_significant_c: Softening of suppository designed to melt at 37C if melting point clearly demonstrated
Dissolution failure for gelatin capsule or gel-coated tablet if unequivocally attributed to cross-linking
accelerated_is_significant_change: Phase separation of a semi-solid dosage form

scenario_A.condition: No significant change at accelerated; little/no change AND little/no variability in long-term + accelerated
scenario_A.without_stats: Y = up to 2X, max X + 12 months

scenario_B_no_stats.condition: No significant change at accelerated; change/variability present; no statistical analysis
scenario_B_no_stats.limit: Y = up to 1.5X, max X + 6 months

scenario_B_with_stats.condition: No significant change at accelerated; change/variability present; statistical analysis performed
scenario_B_with_stats.limit: Y = up to 2X, max X + 12 months

scenario_E_no_stats.condition: Significant change at accelerated; NO significant change at intermediate; no stats

scenario_E_with_stats.condition: Significant change at accelerated; NO significant change at intermediate; stats performed
scenario_E_with_stats.limit: Y = up to 1.5X, max X + 6 months

scenario_D.condition: Significant change at accelerated AND intermediate
scenario_D.limit: No extrapolation; shorter shelf life may be called for

refrigerated.no_sig_change_little_variability: Y = up to 1.5X, max X + 6 months
refrigerated.no_sig_change_with_variability_with_stat: Y = up to 1.5X, max X + 6 months
refrigerated.sig_change_3_to_6_months: No extrapolation; based on long-term data only
refrigerated.sig_change_within_3_months: No extrapolation; shorter shelf life may apply

freezer.approach: Based on long-term data only; test at elevated temp for excursions

below_minus_20.approach: Based on long-term data only; case-by-case

biologicals_2025_draft.limit: 1.5x available long-term data, maximum 12 months beyond
biologicals_2025_draft.conditions: Only for well-characterised frozen biological DS with 95% confidence limit statistical analysis showing no significant/meaningful change
biologicals_2025_draft.requires: Risk-based justification including knowledge of molecule, degradation profile, container/closure

extrapolation_rules.general_rule: Extrapolated shelf life must always be verified by additional long-term data as soon as available

shelf_life_estimation.method: 95% one-sided confidence limit for the mean regression curve intersecting acceptance criterion
shelf_life_estimation.for_decrease: Lower one-sided 95% CL vs lower acceptance criterion
shelf_life_estimation.for_increase: Upper one-sided 95% CL vs upper acceptance criterion
shelf_life_estimation.for_unknown_direction: Two-sided 95% CL vs upper and lower acceptance criteria

batch_poolability.step_1: Test equality of slopes among batches at significance level 0.25
batch_poolability.step_1_reject: Do NOT pool; estimate shelf life per batch using individual slopes/intercepts and pooled MSE; use SHORTEST estimate
batch_poolability.step_2: If slopes not significantly different, test equality of intercepts at significance level 0.25
batch_poolability.step_2_reject: Combine with common slope but individual intercepts; use SHORTEST estimate
batch_poolability.step_3: If neither slopes nor intercepts significantly different: fully pool all data for single shelf life estimate
batch_poolability.note: p > 0.25 means reject the hypothesis that batches differ (i.e., batches are similar enough to pool)

multi_factor_ancova.order: Test slope terms before intercept terms
multi_factor_ancova.higher_order_interactions: Can be excluded if justified as negligible

enhanced_modelling_2025_draft.kinetic_models: Empirical fit of stability data to kinetic functions
enhanced_modelling_2025_draft.mechanistic_models: Based on understanding of degradation pathways
enhanced_modelling_2025_draft.bayesian_statistics: Incorporating prior knowledge from analogous molecules
enhanced_modelling_2025_draft.ai_ml: Recognized as emerging tools; same validation principles apply
enhanced_modelling_2025_draft.no_upper_limit: No upper limit on shelf life prediction using enhanced models if risk-based and scientifically justified

option_1.description: Single source producing output similar to D65/ID65 emission standard (ISO 10977:1993)
option_1.filter: If significant radiation below 320 nm, use appropriate filter to eliminate

option_2.description: Two separate sources used together on same sample
option_2.source_2: Near-UV fluorescent lamp, 320-400 nm, peak 350-370 nm

option_3_2025_draft.note: New in 2025 draft for forced degradation

sample_presentation.solids: Glass or plastic dish, thickness typically NMT 3 mm, transparent cover if needed
sample_presentation.composite_size: Approximately 20 tablets or capsules for analysis

dark_controls.method: Wrapped in aluminum foil, placed alongside exposed sample
dark_controls.analysis: Must be performed concomitantly with exposed sample

drug_substance.forced_degradation: Part of stress testing; conditions at applicant's discretion; single batch
drug_substance.confirmatory: Standardized conditions (1.2M lux hours, 200 Wh/m2); single batch; up to 2 additional if equivocal

sequential_testing: Step 1: Test directly exposed product (outside packaging)
Step 2: If unacceptable, test in immediate pack
Step 3: If still unacceptable, test in marketing pack
Step 4: If still unacceptable, redesign package or reformulate
drug_product.batches: At least one primary batch; up to 2 additional if equivocal

option_1.container: 20 mL colorless ampoule with 10 mL solution

photostability.acceptance_criteria: Not prescriptive; 'acceptable change' defined and justified by applicant within ICH Q1A/Q3A/Q3B specifications

new_dosage_forms_Q1C.definition: Different pharmaceutical product type containing same active substance as an existing approved product, by the same applicant
categories: Different administration route (e.g., oral to parenteral)
New functionality/delivery system (e.g., IR tablet to MR tablet)
Different dosage form same route (e.g., capsule to tablet, solution to suspension)

reduced_data_at_submission.justification: Required; based on existing stability knowledge of active substance

new_dosage_forms_Q1C.protocol: Same as Q1A in principle; same conditions, parameters, design

bracketing_Q1D.definition: Testing only extremes of design factors (strength, container size, fill) at all time points; intermediates assumed represented by extremes
acceptable_when: Multiple strengths of identical or closely related formulations
Same container closure system with either container size OR fill varying (not both)
Comparable stability profiles among strengths demonstrated
not_acceptable_when: Both container size AND fill vary simultaneously
Cannot demonstrate selected items are true extremes
bracketing_Q1D.if_extremes_differ: Intermediates assigned shelf life of least stable extreme

matrixing_Q1D.definition: Testing a selected subset of total possible samples at each time point; different subsets at different time points
rules: Design should be balanced across factor combinations
All combinations tested at initial and final time points
Minimum 3 time points including initial through first 12 months per combination
Accelerated/intermediate: minimum 3 time points per combination

reductions.one_half_reduction: Actual reduction ~15/48 time points (initial, 12-month, and final fully tested)

drug_substance.note: Degradation products from stress not formed under accelerated/long-term need not be further examined

labeling_requirements.drug_substance: Display retest date on container label
labeling_requirements.drug_product: Display expiration date on container label
labeling_requirements.hot_humid_climates: Additional 'Protect from moisture' statement when long-term is 30C/65%RH or higher

stability_commitment.when_required: When long-term data at submission do not cover proposed retest period/shelf life
stability_commitment.scope: Continue long-term studies through proposed period + accelerated through 6 months on 3 production batches
stability_commitment.ongoing_post_approval: At least 1 production batch DS and 1 per strength DP per year through end of retest/shelf life

in_use_stability_2025_draft.scope: Multi-dose products, reconstituted/diluted products, drug-device combinations
in_use_stability_2025_draft.batches: Minimum 2 batches; at least 1 chosen toward end of shelf life
in_use_stability_2025_draft.acceptance_criteria: Product must meet shelf life specifications during in-use period

holding_times_2025_draft.scope: Processing and holding times for intermediates

short_term_storage_2025_draft.definition: Labelled short-term storage condition different from long-term (e.g., refrigerated product at room temperature temporarily)
short_term_storage_2025_draft.requirement: Product must remain within shelf life specifications
short_term_storage_2025_draft.re_evaluation: Not needed unless stability-impacting change occurs

advanced_therapies_2025_draft.scope: Somatic cell therapy, gene therapies, tissue-engineered products
advanced_therapies_2025_draft.shelf_life: Generally based on real-time data; accelerated not predictive for ATMPs
advanced_therapies_2025_draft.additional: Cell bank and viral bank stability; starting materials stability

container_orientation_2025_draft.liquids_semi_solids_suspensions: Both inverted (horizontal) AND upright (vertical) unless worst-case justified

post_approval_changes_2025_draft.shelf_life_extension: Minimum 3 production or primary batches with acceptable stability data
